acceptanceGates: GitHub Actions first run passes on the proposal branch
observed status-check context exactly matches repo-governance / proof before applying ruleset plan
all destructive fixtures remain rejected by expected rule families
same logical input in different order keeps the same result digest
CLI source remains free of rule semantics and core remains free of IO/hidden-state imports
workflow keeps contents:read and no privileged pull_request_target path
decision: Implement the merged repo-governance package strategy as a pure core+port Python standard-library lib, a thin CLI adapter, executable destructive fixtures, and a read-only GitHub Actions gate.
nonGoals: no GitHub ruleset mutation from gov
no network-backed ADR fetch during deterministic evaluation
rollback: remove required-check configuration before deleting or renaming the workflow
revert the implementation PR while retaining proof receipt and source decision refs
keep previous accepted ADR bundle available by digest for comparison
